visual_checks: Look at form controls — does changing the value of a checkbox, radio button, text input, or select element cause an unexpected change of context (page navigation, form submission, or a major layout change)?
Check select/dropdown elements — does choosing an option immediately navigate to a new page or submit a form, without the user pressing a submit button?
Examine checkboxes and radio buttons — does selecting one cause new page sections to appear, content to be removed, or focus to move unexpectedly?
Look for search fields that auto-submit or navigate as the user types, without an explicit search button or clear indication this will happen
Check if any form control has a visible warning or instruction that changing its value will cause a context change (this would make the change acceptable)
pass_conditions: Changing the setting of any form control does not automatically cause a change of context unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component
Select elements that serve as navigation have an explicit 'Go' button rather than auto-navigating on selection change
Form controls that do trigger context changes have clear instructions beforehand explaining this behavior
Auto-updating content areas that respond to input changes (like live search results) update without shifting focus or navigating away
fail_conditions: A select dropdown auto-navigates to a new URL when the user selects an option, with no prior warning and no Go button
Checking a checkbox or selecting a radio button causes the form to auto-submit or the page to navigate
Typing in a text field causes automatic form submission or page navigation without the user pressing Enter or a submit button
Changing a form control causes a new browser window or tab to open without prior warning
A settings toggle causes a full page reload or navigation without advance notice
na_conditions: Page contains no form controls (no inputs, selects, checkboxes, radio buttons, or custom interactive widgets that accept user input)
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Dynamic input behavior could not be tested from static screenshots and DOM snapshots alone
JavaScript event handlers on form controls could not be fully evaluated
common_mistakes: A 'Sort by' or 'Filter by' select dropdown that reloads the page immediately on change instead of using a separate Apply/Go button
A language or region selector that navigates to a different URL on change without warning
Radio buttons that auto-submit a form when a selection is made, bypassing the submit button
Search inputs that navigate to a results page as the user types, without communicating this will happen
A checkbox that immediately enables/disables page sections and shifts focus without prior notice

examples.pass: A 'Sort by' dropdown has options for 'Price', 'Name', 'Date' and a 'Go' button next to it — selecting an option does not reload until the user presses 'Go'
examples.fail: A country-selection <select> element immediately redirects to that country's version of the website when the user picks a country, with no submit button and no prior warning
examples.partial: Most form controls behave predictably, but a 'Number of results per page' dropdown at the bottom of a search results list auto-reloads the page on change with no Go button and no advance notice

plain_meaning: Changing the value or state of a form control (typing, selecting an option, checking a box) must not automatically cause a major change of context unless the user was told in advance that it would. Auto-submitting a form when a select value changes, navigating away when a radio is selected, or jumping to a new page on typing are the classic violations.
scope_applies_to: Form controls whose change event triggers navigation or submission
Select dropdowns that navigate on selection without a submit button
Radio or checkbox inputs that submit the form on change
Text inputs that trigger navigation on keystroke
Custom input widgets whose value change causes a page or window change
scope_does_not_apply_to: Filtering or sorting a visible list on the same page without navigation
Showing/hiding dependent form fields based on a value
Inline validation feedback appearing next to a field
Focus changes within the same form (SC 3.2.1 handles focus, and minor focus moves are not context changes)
Changes the user was clearly advised of in advance (instructions visible before the control)
borderline_calibration: The test is whether a BIG context change (new window, URL change, form submit, major layout shuffle) fires on a setting change.
Revealing dependent fields or updating a widget's own content is NOT a context change.
If the user was warned via visible instructions before the control, PASS even if the behavior fires.
If captured behavior shows selecting an option causes navigation with no prior warning, FAIL.
Prefer Not Enough Evidence if no interaction was actually captured.
